paper_title: Bone Sample Analysis and Species Accumulation

methods.description: Process for pretreating bone samples to prepare them for isotopic and radiocarbon analysis, ensuring the preservation of collagen and accurate dating.

steps.description: The outer surface of the bone samples was first cleaned by a shot blaster, and then 500 mg of the whole bone was taken.
steps.searchable_summary: clean bone samples and extract 500 mg for analysis

steps.description: The samples were then decalcified in 0.5M HCl at room temperature until CO2 effervescence ceased.
Subsequently, 0.1M NaOH was added for 30 min to remove humics.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with 0.1M NaOH to remove humics

steps.description: The NaOH step was followed by a final 0.5M HCl step for 15 min.
The resulting solid was gelatinised at pH3 in a heater block at 75°C for 20 h.
steps.searchable_summary: gelatinise samples at pH3 and 75°C for 20 hours

steps.description: The gelatine was then filtered in an Eeze-Filter™ to remove small (>80 μm) particles.
steps.searchable_summary: filter gelatinised samples to remove particles >80 μm

steps.description: The gelatine was then ultrafiltered with Sartorius 'VivaspinTurbo' 30 KDa ultrafilters.
The samples are lyophilised for 48 h.
All dates were corrected for a residual preparation background estimated from 14C free bone samples.
steps.searchable_summary: correct dates using residual preparation background

steps.description: The preservation of the collagen was ensured by only evaluating samples with a yield of collagen not less than 1%, C:N ratios (between 2.9 and 3.6), together with isotopic values.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate collagen preservation and isotopic values

steps.description: The isotopic values were obtained as a single run at MPI-EVA on a ThermoFinnigan Delta V Advantage isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
After passing the quality criteria, approximately 5-6 mg of collagen was weighed into pre-cleaned tin cups and sent to the Klaus-Tschira-AMS centre.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh collagen and prepare for AMS dating

steps.description: Samples were then combusted in an Elemental Analyser and CO2 converted catalytically to graphite and dated using the MICADAS-AMS.
steps.searchable_summary: combust samples and date using MICADAS-AMS

methods.description: Analysis to determine the reliability of current diversity estimates of mammalian species in Mesolithic zooarchaeological assemblages using a sample-based species accumulation curve.

steps.description: To do this we sourced the number of archaeological sites with in situ and reworked Mesolithic deposits from published sources including the database of Irish Excavation Reports.
steps.searchable_summary: source archaeological site data from published reports

steps.description: Conducted sample-based species accumulation analysis (collectors curve) with a logarithmic function on the number of mammalian species detected.
steps.searchable_summary: perform species accumulation analysis using a logarithmic function
